## Active Project Conventions (from AGENTS.md)

| Convention | Rule |
|------------|------|
| **TypeScript** | `interface` for public types, `type` for unions/aliases; camelCase variables; PascalCase React/components; strict:true |
| **React/Next.js** | Functional components; `use client` only when needed; Server Components default; Tailwind CSS |
| **API REST** | `/api/` prefix; `{ success, data?, error? }` responses; Zod validation; JWT Bearer auth |
| **Auth Roles** | ADMIN (full), VENDEDOR (clients+loans), CLIENTE (own loans) |
| **Database** | Prisma migrations in `packages/database/prisma/migrations/`; use `prisma.$transaction` for multi-op |
| **Testing** | Unit: `*.test.ts` co-located (Vitest); Integration: `src/routes/*.test.ts` (Supertest); E2E: `apps/web/e2e/*.test.ts` (Playwright) |
| **Commits** | Conventional Commits: `feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, etc. |
| **Package manager** | pnpm (>=8.0.0) |
| **Node** | >=20.0.0 |

## Contract

**Delegator use only.** Any agent that launches subagents reads this registry to resolve compact rules, then injects matching rule text into subagent prompts under `## Project Standards (auto-resolved)`.

Subagents still read their assigned executor/phase skill. During normal runtime, they do **not** independently discover or load additional project/user `SKILL.md` files or this registry; project/user rules arrive pre-digested. Explicit fallback loading is degraded self-healing and must be reported in `skill_resolution` as `fallback-registry` or `fallback-path`.
